LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS CAITLIN BOURNE, EMILIO MELENSDEZ AND CORY WRIGHT'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS'ANSWER TO COMPLAINT - PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT'S THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SUFFICIENT SUPPORTIVE FACTS. THE COURT STRIKES DEFENDANT'S FIRST, EIGHTH, NINTH, AND ELEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BECAUSE THEY DO NOT RAISE ANY NEW MATTER. THE DEFENSES MERELY ALLEGE THAT SOME ESSENTIAL ALLEGATION OF THE COMPLAINT IS NOT TRUE, WHICH DEFENDANTS HAVE PUT IN ISSUE UNDER A GENERAL DENIAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2014 Continued To Aug-13-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ASHLEY BALTAZAR OF THE SOMMERS EMPLOYMENT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 524-2860 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS CAITLIN BOURNE, EMILIO MELENSDEZ and CORY WRIGHT's DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS'ANSWER TO COMPLAINT - Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendant's third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh affirmative defenses is sustained with leave to amend to plead sufficient supportive facts. The court strikes Defendant's first, eighth, ninth, and eleventh affirmative defenses because they do not raise any new matter. The defenses merely allege that some essential allegation of the complaint is not true, which Defendants have put in issue under a general denial. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
